title: sonchus littoralis
intro: Sonchus littoralis, also known as sea sowthistle, is a biennial or perennial plant species in the dandelion genus, belonging to the sunflower family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. Sonchus littoralis has been reported to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-cancer, and anti-diabetic effects, as well as the ability to lower cholesterol levels and improve liver function. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
